parameters.subject: =Application for {{$json.jobTitle}} - {{$json.jobCode}}
parameters.message:
=Dear {{$json.hrName}},

I came across the {{$json.jobTitle}} opportunity and wanted to express my strong interest in the role.

With extensive experience across digital growth strategy, performance marketing, analytics, AI-driven initiatives, and full-funnel campaign execution, I believe my background aligns very well with the requirements of this position.

{{$json.whyFitOrNot}}

Some of the relevant skills and technologies from my background include:
{{$json.keyMatchingSkills}}

I have attached my resume for your consideration and would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience could contribute to your team.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Prashant Kumar
+44 7435 773972
Kumar.Prashant.UK@gmail.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/prashantkumarltd

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are an expert AI resume parser and recruitment intelligence assistant. Always return STRICTLY VALID JSON ONLY. Never return markdown, explanations, or extra text."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Analyze the following resume carefully and extract structured recruitment intelligence.

RULES:
1. Return ONLY valid JSON.
2. Fill every field appropriately.
3. Arrays should contain concise meaningful entries.
4. preferredRoles must contain realistic matching job roles.
5. businessImpact must contain measurable outcomes and achievements.
6. workExperience should contain concise summaries.
7. projects can be inferred from experience if explicit projects are not available.
8. Keep resumeSummary concise but useful for AI job matching.
9. Do not leave fields empty unless absolutely unavailable.

Return ONLY this exact JSON structure:

{
  "resumeSummary": "",
  "coreSkills": [],
  "technicalSkills": [],
  "softSkills": [],
  "toolsAndTechnologies": [],
  "industryDomains": [],
  "workExperience": [],
  "projects": [],
  "certifications": [],
  "education": [],
  "leadershipExperience": "",
  "achievements": [],
  "keywords": [],
  "preferredRoles": [],
  "businessImpact": [],
  "aiExperience": "",
  "marketingExperience": "",
  "analyticsExperience": ""
}

RESUME:
{{$json.cleanedResume}}"
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.2
}

json.description:
Your new companyAre you a data‑driven Performance Marketing professional looking to take ownership of strategy and scale growth across digital channels? Our client, a high‑growth organisation within the Media/Tech Space, is looking for a confident and commercially minded Performance Marketing Manager to join their dynamic team.Please note: This role does not offer sponsorship. Your new roleAs Performance Marketing Manager, you will play a pivotal role in driving customer acquisition, optimising campaign performance, and shaping the digital marketing roadmap. Working closely with product, analytics, and commercial teams, you will deliver measurable ROI across paid social, paid search, display, and emerging digital channels.Key Responsibilities:Own the end‑to‑end performance marketing strategy across paid channelsManage campaign planning, execution, optimisation, and reportingAnalyse data to drive actionable insights and improve ROIDevelop A/B tests to improve ad effectiveness and conversionWork with cross‑functional teams to align marketing activity with brand and product strategyManage external agencies and platform relationships where requiredWhat you'll need to succeedExperience within the Media or Technology sector — essentialProven success delivering measurable growth across paid digital channelsStrong analytical mindset with hands‑on experience using key marketing and analytics toolsSolid understanding of attribution, customer funnels, and digital best practiceStrong communication skills and the ability to influence stakeholdersWhat you’ll get in returnA highly visible role with the opportunity to make a lasting impact on a global brand.Competitive salary plus benefits.A collaborative, design-led culture that values creativity, curiosity, and innovation.The chance to shape the future of a brand trusted by the world’s leading hospitality names.
Your new company
Are you a data‑driven Performance Marketing professional looking to take ownership of strategy and scale growth across digital channels? Our client, a high‑growth organisation within the Media/Tech Space, is looking for a confident and commercially minded Performance Marketing Manager to join their dynamic team.
Your new role
Key Responsibilities:
Own the end‑to‑end performance marketing strategy across paid channels
Manage campaign planning, execution, optimisation, and reporting
Analyse data to drive actionable insights and improve ROI
Develop A/B tests to improve ad effectiveness and conversion
Work with cross‑functional teams to align marketing activity with brand and product strategy
Manage external agencies and platform relationships where required
What you'll need to succeed
Experience within the Media or Technology sector — essential
Proven success delivering measurable growth across paid digital channels
Strong analytical mindset with hands‑on experience using key marketing and analytics tools
Solid understanding of attribution, customer funnels, and digital best practice
Strong communication skills and the ability to influence stakeholders
What you’ll get in return
A highly visible role with the opportunity to make a lasting impact on a global brand.
Competitive salary plus benefits.
A collaborative, design-led culture that values creativity, curiosity, and innovation.
The chance to shape the future of a brand trusted by the world’s leading hospitality names.

Your new company Our client is a well‑established and leading sports publisher with a loyal consumer audience and an expanding digital footprint. They produce high-quality content across web, print and events, and are now investing in new revenue streams, subscription models, and technology-led experiences. They are recognised for delivering trusted, insight‑led sports journalism and engaging storytelling that resonates with fans across multiple generations.With continued investment in digital innovation, they are evolving their platforms to enhance audience experience and deepen community engagement. This is an exciting opportunity to join a brand undergoing meaningful growth, where marketing plays a central role in shaping its future direction.Please note: this role does not offer sponsorship.Your new role As a Marketing Executive, you will play a key role in driving B2C audience engagement, digital acquisition and subscription growth across multiple channels. This is a hands-on, varied role that suits someone confident managing multiple workstreams, comfortable with data, and excited to work across emerging digital tools and platforms.You’ll be joining at an exciting point of growth as the brand diversifies into new revenue areas including subscription products and trade shows. This role offers real autonomy, visibility across the business, and the chance to influence marketing strategy..Key responsibilities include:Deliver digital marketing campaigns across email, social, paid and web to support audience growth and subscriptions.Analyse campaign performance, user behaviour and engagement metrics to inform optimisation and reporting.Support subscription marketing and retention activity, helping develop new customer journeys and lifecycle programmes.Contribute to marketing around trade shows, consumer events and brand activations.Manage content updates, newsletters and CMS activity in collaboration with editorial teams.Work across new digital tools and emerging technologies to enhance customer experience.Work independently to manage deadlines, juggle multiple tasks and adapt quickly to changing priorities.What you'll need to succeed : Experience within publishing is essential.Strong digital marketing skills (email, social, analytics, CMS).A data-driven mindset with the ability to interpret and act on insights.Confidence working autonomously and managing several projects at once.Curiosity for new technologies and an eagerness to test, learn and optimise.A proactive, “joiner” attitude — someone who enjoys contributing broadly within a lean team.This role would suit a marketer with approximately 3–6 years’ experience, looking to step into a position with more ownership and variety.What you'll get in return Opportunity to work across diverse campaigns in a fast‑moving, creative environmentExposure to senior stakeholdersHybrid workingSupportive, collaborative team cultureExcellent scope for professional development and progressionSalary £35,000–£40,000 depending on experience.Opportunity to learn, develop and shape marketing activity for a respected sports publisher.What you need to do now  If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
Your new company
Our client is a well‑established and leading sports publisher with a loyal consumer audience and an expanding digital footprint. They produce high-quality content across web, print and events, and are now investing in new revenue streams, subscription models, and technology-led experiences. They are recognised for delivering trusted, insight‑led sports journalism and engaging storytelling that resonates with fans across multiple generations.With continued investment in digital innovation, they are evolving their platforms to enhance audience experience and deepen community engagement. This is an exciting opportunity to join a brand undergoing meaningful growth, where marketing plays a central role in shaping its future direction.
Your new role
As a Marketing Executive, you will play a key role in driving B2C audience engagement, digital acquisition and subscription growth across multiple channels. This is a hands-on, varied role that suits someone confident managing multiple workstreams, comfortable with data, and excited to work across emerging digital tools and platforms.
You’ll be joining at an exciting point of growth as the brand diversifies into new revenue areas including subscription products and trade shows. This role offers real autonomy, visibility across the business, and the chance to influence marketing strategy.
.
Key responsibilities include:
Deliver digital marketing campaigns across email, social, paid and web to support audience growth and subscriptions.
Analyse campaign performance, user behaviour and engagement metrics to inform optimisation and reporting.
Support subscription marketing and retention activity, helping develop new customer journeys and lifecycle programmes.
Contribute to marketing around trade shows, consumer events and brand activations.
Manage content updates, newsletters and CMS activity in collaboration with editorial teams.
Work across new digital tools and emerging technologies to enhance customer experience.
Work independently to manage deadlines, juggle multiple tasks and adapt quickly to changing priorities.
What you'll need to succeed :
Experience within publishing is essential.
Strong digital marketing skills (email, social, analytics, CMS).
A data-driven mindset with the ability to interpret and act on insights.
Confidence working autonomously and managing several projects at once.
Curiosity for new technologies and an eagerness to test, learn and optimise.
A proactive, “joiner” attitude — someone who enjoys contributing broadly within a lean team.
This role would suit a marketer with approximately 3–6 years’ experience, looking to step into a position with more ownership and variety.
This role would suit a marketer with approximately 3–6 years’ experience, looking to step into a position with more ownership and variety.
What you'll get in return
Opportunity to work across diverse campaigns in a fast‑moving, creative environment
Exposure to senior stakeholders
Hybrid working
Supportive, collaborative team culture
Excellent scope for professional development and progression
Salary £35,000–£40,000 depending on experience.
Opportunity to learn, develop and shape marketing activity for a respected sports publisher.
What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

json.jobTitle: B2B Senior Marketing Campaign Executive (on-site role)
json.description:
I’m delighted to partner with an innovative construction business that’s growing fast and are now looking to appoint a B2B Senior Marketing Campaign Executive to join their team.If you’re a creative marketer who enjoys working with technical products and knows how to turn complex ideas into clear, compelling stories, this role is for you!If you’re the kind of marketer who loves owning campaigns end-to-end, turning complex technical detail into clear, compelling content, and proving impact through leads, conversion and performance optimisation — this role is for you!You’ll operate as a senior member of a small marketing team, taking full ownership of campaign delivery, performance, and continuous optimisation — and you’ll see the commercial results of your work in action. About the role.You’ll manage integrated campaigns from planning → execution → optimisation → reporting, with clear commercial outcomes:Build and deliver multichannel campaigns across digital, email, social, and printCreate bold, persona-led messaging that convertsTranslate technical product information into clear, engaging marketing contentOwn website messaging and conversion: update/optimise via CMS using SEO + UX best practicePartner closely with Sales, Business Development and Technical teams to align marketing with the pipeline.Produce high-quality content (case studies, blogs, email sequences, video)Design and edit assets using Adobe Suite / Canva and video toolsTrack KPIs, analyse performance, and continuously improve results (MQLs, engagement, conversions)Run campaigns and reporting through HubSpot (automation, workflows, journeys, performance)The CandidateWe’re looking for a proven campaign owner — someone who can show results, not just activity:Minimum 5+ years’ experience managing integrated campaigns in a technical B2B product environment (manufacturing/industrial/engineering), ideally constructionStrong grasp of lead gen funnels, buying journeys and conversion optimisationHands-on channel experience: SEO, PPC, email, LinkedInExperience with marketing automation/CRM (HubSpot strongly preferred)Confident turning complex technical detail into commercially effective marketingComfortable managing multiple campaigns and priorities at paceWhat success looks like in this roleHubSpot campaigns and workflows run confidently and independently.Website performance improves measurably (traffic quality, time on page, conversion actions)Campaign reporting clearly shows what’s working, by channel and audience.A steady flow of qualified leads aligned to pipeline growthWhat you’ll getCompetitive salary DOE + monthly bonus31 days holidayBuy/sell holiday schemeFree lunch everydayBirthday voucherPension schemeHealth insurance scheme (incl. discounted gym membership/appointment paybacks)Full-time on-site role with flexible start/finish timesIf you’re ready to shape bold campaigns, elevate digital journeys, and bring fresh ideas to a growing team, we’d love to hear from you.Early applications are strongly encouraged. If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.  If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
I’m delighted to partner with an innovative construction business that’s growing fast and are now looking to appoint a B2B Senior Marketing Campaign Executive to join their team.
If you’re a creative marketer who enjoys working with technical products and knows how to turn complex ideas into clear, compelling stories, this role is for you!
If you’re the kind of marketer who loves owning campaigns end-to-end, turning complex technical detail into clear, compelling content, and proving impact through leads, conversion and performance optimisation — this role is for you!
You’ll operate as a senior member of a small marketing team, taking full ownership of campaign delivery, performance, and continuous optimisation — and you’ll see the commercial results of your work in action.
About the role.
You’ll manage integrated campaigns from planning → execution → optimisation → reporting, with clear commercial outcomes:
Build and deliver multichannel campaigns across digital, email, social, and print
Create bold, persona-led messaging that converts
Translate technical product information into clear, engaging marketing content
Own website messaging and conversion: update/optimise via CMS using SEO + UX best practice
Partner closely with Sales, Business Development and Technical teams to align marketing with the pipeline.
Produce high-quality content (case studies, blogs, email sequences, video)
Design and edit assets using Adobe Suite / Canva and video tools
Track KPIs, analyse performance, and continuously improve results (MQLs, engagement, conversions)
Run campaigns and reporting through HubSpot (automation, workflows, journeys, performance)
The Candidate
We’re looking for a proven campaign owner — someone who can show results, not just activity:
Minimum 5+ years’ experience managing integrated campaigns in a technical B2B product environment (manufacturing/industrial/engineering), ideally construction
Strong grasp of lead gen funnels, buying journeys and conversion optimisation
Hands-on channel experience: SEO, PPC, email, LinkedIn
Experience with marketing automation/CRM (HubSpot strongly preferred)
Confident turning complex technical detail into commercially effective marketing
Comfortable managing multiple campaigns and priorities at pace
What success looks like in this role
HubSpot campaigns and workflows run confidently and independently.
Website performance improves measurably (traffic quality, time on page, conversion actions)
Campaign reporting clearly shows what’s working, by channel and audience.
A steady flow of qualified leads aligned to pipeline growth
What you’ll get
Competitive salary DOE + monthly bonus
31 days holiday
Buy/sell holiday scheme
Free lunch everyday
Birthday voucher
Pension scheme
Health insurance scheme (incl. discounted gym membership/appointment paybacks)
Full-time on-site role with flexible start/finish times
If you’re ready to shape bold campaigns, elevate digital journeys, and bring fresh ideas to a growing team, we’d love to hear from you.
Early applications are strongly encouraged.
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.
If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

json.jobTitle: Growth & Content Marketing Manager – Sports Media
json.description:
Your new company A premium sports media and events company is seeking a Growth & Content Marketing Manager to lead the organisation’s content and growth strategy, with a strong focus on campaign execution and commercial performance. Although titled as a manager, this is the most senior marketing role in the business, responsible for shaping strategy and driving growth across publishing, subscriptions, e‑commerce and events. This opportunity is ideal for an ambitious marketer ready to step up, combining strategic leadership with hands‑on delivery in a creative, fast‑paced environment. The company lives and breathes sport, making this an excellent fit for someone who is genuinely sports‑obsessed.*This role does not offer sponsorship*Your new role You’ll lead on content growth strategy, campaign development and performance marketing across all revenue channels — including digital subscriptions, premium editorial content, events, and a curated retail offering. Working closely with editorial, design, technical and customer teams, you’ll set the roadmap, own the results, and deliver meaningful commercial impact. This is an ideal role for someone ready to step up, operate autonomously, and grow into a wider leadership remit over time. Your responsibilities will include:Leading the company’s growth strategy, owning how the brand scales subscriptions, audience and revenue.Plan and deliver end‑to‑end marketing campaigns, combining creativity, storytelling and strong brand execution.Use data to build and continually optimise the marketing funnel, identifying where and how growth can be accelerated.Run targeted acquisition, retention and CRM campaigns, using segmentation, testing and behavioural insight.Analyse performance across subscriptions, events and e‑commerce to identify trends, opportunities and performance gaps.Create clear reporting dashboards and track KPIs that link directly to commercial outcomes.Translate complex data into actionable insights to shape campaign strategy and business decisions.Work closely with editorial and creative teams to develop content‑led, visually engaging campaigns.Combine creative judgement with analytical rigour to ensure campaigns are both impactful and measurable.Support platform improvements and digital upgrades to enhance user journey and conversion.What you'll need to succeed Unfortunately, this role does not offer sponsorship, and to apply you must meet the following requirements:A strong understanding of publishing models and recurring revenue — essential.Sports obsession and experience in the D2C sports environment is a plus. Experience working within subscriptions‑focused, content‑led or media environments.Ability to balance creative campaign thinking with high analytical capability.Comfortable working independently or collaboratively within a small team.A proactive, hands‑on approach — able to lead strategy and deliver campaigns.What you'll get in return A creative and relaxed working environment.High standards, premium output, and a quality over quantity ethos.Competitive salary and benefits package.The opportunity to eventually build out a full marketing team.Hybrid working policy What you need to do now  If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
Your new company
A premium sports media and events company is seeking a Growth & Content Marketing Manager to lead the organisation’s content and growth strategy, with a strong focus on campaign execution and commercial performance. Although titled as a manager, this is the most senior marketing role in the business, responsible for shaping strategy and driving growth across publishing, subscriptions, e‑commerce and events. This opportunity is ideal for an ambitious marketer ready to step up, combining strategic leadership with hands‑on delivery in a creative, fast‑paced environment. The company lives and breathes sport, making this an excellent fit for someone who is genuinely sports‑obsessed.
Your new role
You’ll lead on content growth strategy, campaign development and performance marketing across all revenue channels — including digital subscriptions, premium editorial content, events, and a curated retail offering. Working closely with editorial, design, technical and customer teams, you’ll set the roadmap, own the results, and deliver meaningful commercial impact. This is an ideal role for someone ready to step up, operate autonomously, and grow into a wider leadership remit over time. Your responsibilities will include:
Leading the company’s growth strategy, owning how the brand scales subscriptions, audience and revenue.
Plan and deliver end‑to‑end marketing campaigns, combining creativity, storytelling and strong brand execution.
Use data to build and continually optimise the marketing funnel, identifying where and how growth can be accelerated.
Run targeted acquisition, retention and CRM campaigns, using segmentation, testing and behavioural insight.
Analyse performance across subscriptions, events and e‑commerce to identify trends, opportunities and performance gaps.
Create clear reporting dashboards and track KPIs that link directly to commercial outcomes.
Translate complex data into actionable insights to shape campaign strategy and business decisions.
Work closely with editorial and creative teams to develop content‑led, visually engaging campaigns.
Combine creative judgement with analytical rigour to ensure campaigns are both impactful and measurable.
Support platform improvements and digital upgrades to enhance user journey and conversion.
What you'll need to succeed
Unfortunately, this role does not offer sponsorship, and to apply you must meet the following requirements:
A strong understanding of publishing models and recurring revenue — essential.
Sports obsession and experience in the D2C sports environment is a plus.
Experience working within subscriptions‑focused, content‑led or media environments.
Ability to balance creative campaign thinking with high analytical capability.
Comfortable working independently or collaboratively within a small team.
A proactive, hands‑on approach — able to lead strategy and deliver campaigns.
What you'll get in return
A creative and relaxed working environment.
High standards, premium output, and a quality over quantity ethos.
Competitive salary and benefits package.
The opportunity to eventually build out a full marketing team.
Hybrid working policy
What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

Your new company You’ll be joining a values‑led organisation recognised for delivering exceptional hospitality, food service and customer experience across a national, multi-site contract. The business is known for its innovation, strong partnerships and commitment to creating engaging environments for customers and clients alike. You’ll be part of a collaborative team that is passionate about service, creativity and making a measurable impact.Your new role As the Marketing Manager for a major national contract (12‑month maternity cover), you will lead the full marketing strategy and execution across a diverse estate. This is a high‑impact role responsible for driving customer engagement, participation, brand perception and commercial performance.You will act as the key marketing link between internal teams and senior client stakeholders, ensuring all communications, campaigns and initiatives are strategically aligned and commercially effective. Your work will span strategic planning, campaign development, content creation, trade marketing, supplier partnerships, event activation, reporting and innovation delivery.You’ll oversee annual and quarterly marketing plans, manage contract-wide communications, deliver site engagement initiatives, support industry events, and produce regular performance reporting. This is a role that blends strategy, creativity and hands‑on delivery.What you'll need to succeed Proven experience delivering marketing campaigns, events and multi‑channel communications.Background in hospitality, retail, food service or customer‑facing environments is highly advantageous.Ability to work confidently with multiple stakeholders, including senior leadership and client teams.Strong commercial acumen with experience analysing customer insight, sales data and campaign performance.Excellent written and verbal communication skills across a variety of platforms.Highly organised, able to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work to tight deadlines.Proficiency in Microsoft Office; Adobe Creative Suite (especially InDesign) is desirable.A proactive, creative, solutions‑focused mindset with the ability to thrive in a fast‑paced environment.What you'll get in return You’ll have the opportunity to shape and lead the marketing direction of a flagship national contract, gaining exposure to senior stakeholders and contributing to a vibrant culture of innovation. In this influential role, you’ll drive meaningful engagement, develop award‑worthy campaigns, and see the real impact of your work across high-profile estate.You’ll also join a supportive, collaborative team environment that values creativity, autonomy and personal development - offering variety, visibility and the chance to make a measurable difference over the contract period.What you need to do now If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.  If this job isn't quite right for you but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.
Proven experience delivering marketing campaigns, events and multi‑channel communications.
Background in hospitality, retail, food service or customer‑facing environments is highly advantageous.
Ability to work confidently with multiple stakeholders, including senior leadership and client teams.
Strong commercial acumen with experience analysing customer insight, sales data and campaign performance.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills across a variety of platforms.
Highly organised, able to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work to tight deadlines.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office; Adobe Creative Suite (especially InDesign) is desirable.
A proactive, creative, solutions‑focused mindset with the ability to thrive in a fast‑paced environment.

Your new company A hyper‑growth, social‑first consumer brand creating highly shareable products enjoyed by millions worldwide. Already achieving hundreds of millions of organic views, the business is scaling rapidly by building content that spreads natively across platforms rather than relying on paid media alone. This is a fast‑moving, founder‑led environment where creativity, experimentation, and speed matter. The company operates with a small, nonperforming team and gives real ownership to people who want to build systems, not just execute tasks.Your new role You will own the end‑to‑end strategy for driving organic views across all owned social channels. Your mission is to turn content into a scalable growth engine - building repeatable viral formats, testing new creative approaches, and ensuring learnings directly influence wider marketing performance.You will set the creative direction across multiple brands and SKUs, lead a growing team, and design the systems that allow high‑volume, high‑quality content to ship consistently. You’ll manage experimentation (including emerging formats, AI‑generated content, memes, and platform‑native trends), while holding clear ownership of view targets across a large portfolio of social accounts.The role sits at the centre of the business, supplying performance signals to paid and partner marketing teams, informing inventory and revenue decisions, and ultimately ensuring organic attention turns into measurable commercial impact.What you'll need to succeed Proven experience building and scaling organic social ecosystems, with a strong intuition for viral content.A track record of setting creative direction while maintaining operational rigour at high output volumes.Deep understanding of experimentation: forming hypotheses, running tests, documenting results, and scaling winners.Strong leadership skills, with experience managing and developing creative or marketing teams.Comfort working with data - interpreting performance signals, building reporting frameworks, and linking views to revenue outcomes.A proactive, resourceful mindset and confidence taking ownership of both execution and results.A genuine obsession with internet culture, emerging formats, and how content behaves across platforms.What you'll get in return A high‑impact leadership role with true ownership over organic growth strategy.Creative freedom to experiment, test, and scale ideas at pace.A collaborative, ambitious team environment where strong performance is visible and valued.Hybrid working with a central London office.Competitive benefits including generous holiday allowance, pension scheme, regular team events, and product perks.The opportunity to shape a function that directly drives the company’s growth trajectory through organic attentionWhat you need to do now If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.  If this job isn't quite right for you but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.
Proven experience building and scaling organic social ecosystems, with a strong intuition for viral content.
A track record of setting creative direction while maintaining operational rigour at high output volumes.
Deep understanding of experimentation: forming hypotheses, running tests, documenting results, and scaling winners.
Strong leadership skills, with experience managing and developing creative or marketing teams.
Comfort working with data - interpreting performance signals, building reporting frameworks, and linking views to revenue outcomes.
A proactive, resourceful mindset and confidence taking ownership of both execution and results.
A genuine obsession with internet culture, emerging formats, and how content behaves across platforms.
A high‑impact leadership role with true ownership over organic growth strategy.
Creative freedom to experiment, test, and scale ideas at pace.
A collaborative, ambitious team environment where strong performance is visible and valued.
Hybrid working with a central London office.
Competitive benefits including generous holiday allowance, pension scheme, regular team events, and product perks.
The opportunity to shape a function that directly drives the company’s growth trajectory through organic attention

json.jobTitle: Brand and Content Marketing Manager - Tech
json.description:
Your new company You'll be joining a rapidly scaling AI‑driven Tech business headquartered in London, building cutting‑edge systems that power some of the world's most sophisticated financial institutions. This organisation is redefining how high‑stakes work gets done across banking, investing, and professional services through advanced automation, intelligent workflows, and enterprise‑grade creative execution. The team is growing fast, backed by the worlds top‑tier investors, and trusted by major global financial institutions who rely on the company's technology to operate with greater speed, precision, and insight. With a culture built around creative excellence, fast iteration, and ambitious product vision, this is a rare opportunity to shape and elevate the brand story of a category‑defining platform.Your new role As the Content Marketing Manager, you will take full ownership of the end‑to‑end content engine – from concept to execution to distribution. This is a hands‑on, high‑autonomy role where your creative instincts, editorial judgment, and ability to deliver quickly will directly shape how the company communicates with clients, investors, and the wider market.You will:Own full‑cycle content production, including thought leadership, written content, video assets, founder storytelling, product narratives, and multi‑channel campaigns.Translate complex, technical ideas into high‑quality, credible, premium content tailored for enterprise financial audiences.Coordinate shoots, freelancers, designers, editors, and internal stakeholders to ensure fast, polished, repeatable output.Produce content for LinkedIn, website, product launches, recruitment campaigns, and brand moments.Repurpose long‑form work into high‑performing social assets, clips, snippets, and micro‑stories.Act as a hands‑on creator, comfortable filming, editing, writing, and building assets with minimal oversight.Uphold high editorial taste, ensuring the brand consistently feels intelligent, premium, and world‑class.What you'll need to succeed 2-4 years of experience as a content marketer or creative contributor - not entry‑level, but still early‑career with clear upward trajectory.A background in high‑quality startups, B2B SaaS, fintech, consulting, or strong creative agencies.Demonstrated ability to produce professional‑grade content for technical, enterprise, or financial audiences.Strong organisational skills - able to manage content workflows, external creatives, and fast‑moving production schedules.Comfort being both the strategist and the execution arm: writing, scripting, filming, editing, producing.High creative standards and strong editorial taste; you know what "premium" looks and feels like.Independence: you can take a brief, run with it, and deliver polished work without hand‑holding.What you'll get in return The chance to define and scale the content function at one of the most exciting AI‑driven technology companies in Europe.A highly visible role where your work directly shapes brand perception and market influence across global financial institutions.Exposure to founders, product teams, and executive stakeholders who deeply value creativity and clear communication.Fast‑paced growth, autonomy, and the opportunity to build a portfolio of high‑impact work recognised across the industry.A culture that celebrates excellence, trusts its people, and invests in craft, storytelling, and creative innovation.Competitive compensation, career progression, and the opportunity to grow alongside a company scaling at speed.What you need to do now  If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
Your new company
Your new role
As the Content Marketing Manager, you will take full ownership of the end‑to‑end content engine – from concept to execution to distribution. This is a hands‑on, high‑autonomy role where your creative instincts, editorial judgment, and ability to deliver quickly will directly shape how the company communicates with clients, investors, and the wider market.
Own full‑cycle content production, including thought leadership, written content, video assets, founder storytelling, product narratives, and multi‑channel campaigns.
Translate complex, technical ideas into high‑quality, credible, premium content tailored for enterprise financial audiences.
Coordinate shoots, freelancers, designers, editors, and internal stakeholders to ensure fast, polished, repeatable output.
Produce content for LinkedIn, website, product launches, recruitment campaigns, and brand moments.
Repurpose long‑form work into high‑performing social assets, clips, snippets, and micro‑stories.
Act as a hands‑on creator, comfortable filming, editing, writing, and building assets with minimal oversight.
Uphold high editorial taste, ensuring the brand consistently feels intelligent, premium, and world‑class.
What you'll need to succeed
2-4 years of experience as a content marketer or creative contributor - not entry‑level, but still early‑career with clear upward trajectory.

Demonstrated ability to produce professional‑grade content for technical, enterprise, or financial audiences.
Strong organisational skills - able to manage content workflows, external creatives, and fast‑moving production schedules.
Comfort being both the strategist and the execution arm: writing, scripting, filming, editing, producing.
High creative standards and strong editorial taste; you know what "premium" looks and feels like.
Independence: you can take a brief, run with it, and deliver polished work without hand‑holding.
What you'll get in return
The chance to define and scale the content function at one of the most exciting AI‑driven technology companies in Europe.
A highly visible role where your work directly shapes brand perception and market influence across global financial institutions.
Exposure to founders, product teams, and executive stakeholders who deeply value creativity and clear communication.
Fast‑paced growth, autonomy, and the opportunity to build a portfolio of high‑impact work recognised across the industry.
A culture that celebrates excellence, trusts its people, and invests in craft, storytelling, and creative innovation.
Competitive compensation, career progression, and the opportunity to grow alongside a company scaling at speed.

across paid media, organic social, influencer activity, PR, events, and marketing mix modelling - to create clear, actionable storylines for key stakeholders.You will:Consolidate data and insights to provide compelling narratives on campaign performance.Translate complex or ambiguous information into simple, digestible insights for a broad audience.Build strong relationships with cross-functional teams including product, marketing development, commercial marketing and creative strategy.Drive a culture of measurement excellence by shaping SMART objectives, performance benchmarks, and consistent frameworks.Project manage global campaign reviews from objective-setting to final results presentation.Support the management of Post Campaign Analyses with media agency partners, ensuring clear communication, timely feedback, and structured delivery.What you'll need to succeed A strong analytical, data-led mindset with intermediate to advanced Excel skills.Proven experience turning complex data into clear, confident communication and insights.A collaborative approach and the ability to thrive in cross-functional environments.Experience within FMCG, brand-side marketing, or a media agency (brand knowledge is particularly valued).Familiarity working across Owned, Earned, Shared and Paid channels, with exposure to Marketing Mix Modelling being advantageous.Experience working within a commercial business environment is beneficial.What you'll get in return The chance to work within a high-profile team shaping marketing effectiveness across a global organisation.Exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic decision-making.A collaborative culture where insight-led thinking is championed.Professional growth through hands-on ownership of high-value global projects and campaign measurement frameworksWhat you need to do now If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now. With a successful direct‑to‑consumer Shopify store generating c.£710k in net sales last year, alongside a well‑developed Amazon Seller operation, the business is focused on scaling its digital channels sustainably and profitably.Operating in a collaborative SME environment, the company works with specialist agencies for areas such as PPC, Amazon advertising, and web development, while retaining commercial ownership and strategic direction in‑house. About the roleAs a Sales and Marketing Manager, you will be reporting to the Marketing Lead and working as a project lead for various marketing initiatives, overseeing the end-to-end management. This will require a tight alignment with the cross-functional, commercial teams and vendors to ensure project outcomes meet the pre-defined goals in terms of quality and uptake, timeline and budget. The initiatives encompass various customer engagements, multichannel content development and dissemination plans in line with the overall brand strategy and messaging objectives. The key collaborating functions include Medical Affairs, Marketing for local affiliates, Compliance, Digital Strategy and Operations, Congress Management, Corporate Communications and Patient Affairs.This role requires a strong sense of urgency, ownership and a problem-solving mindset, backed by demonstrable skills in communication, multitasking and teamwork.Tell me more, tell me more…Our client is currently looking for a new recruit to join their team. Please read on! You can also ask our friendly recruitment team any questions you may have about the role, between 09.00am till 17.00pm Monday to Friday.Shifts:Monday to Friday (37.5 hours)Experience:A relevant bachelor’s degree such as Life Sciences, Communications or Business AdministrationAbout 5 years of experience in theBiopharmaceutical industry, with a preference for a background in marketing, demonstrating a successful track record in cross-functional project managementDemonstrable exposure to international, multiple brands and scientific fluency will be highly valued.Strong collaborative skills, with the ability to engage effectively with stakeholders at various levels of the organisation to cultivate productive working relationshipsProven ability to coordinate multiple projects and deliver high-quality results in complex markets and therapeutic areasExcellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to convey information clearly and effectivelyAdaptable and agile in response to change and new knowledge areasSense of urgency, ownership and problem-solving mindsetStrategic Thinking and Results-Driven: Capable of formulating strategic plans with a clear focus on achieving measurable resultsWhat’s in it for you? –Our client loves to reward their people for doing a great job.This is a contract for 24 months.A daily pay rate of IR35 £350.00 daily day Umbrella or £195.53 PAYE.This role provides hybrid working access based in Central London.Next StepsOnce you’ve applied, one of our friendly recruitment consultants will give you a call and talk you through the screening process.If your application is successful, you’ll be involved in a live virtual interview with one of our client’s hiring managers to get to know you better.We look forward to speaking to you!
About the role
As a Sales and Marketing Manager, you will be reporting to the Marketing Lead and working as a project lead for various marketing initiatives, overseeing the end-to-end management. This will require a tight alignment with the cross-functional, commercial teams and vendors to ensure project outcomes meet the pre-defined goals in terms of quality and uptake, timeline and budget. The initiatives encompass various customer engagements, multichannel content development and dissemination plans in line with the overall brand strategy and messaging objectives. The key collaborating functions include Medical Affairs, Marketing for local affiliates, Compliance, Digital Strategy and Operations, Congress Management, Corporate Communications and Patient Affairs.
This role requires a strong sense of urgency, ownership and a problem-solving mindset, backed by demonstrable skills in communication, multitasking and teamwork.
Tell me more, tell me more…
Our client is currently looking for a new recruit to join their team. Please read on! You can also ask our friendly recruitment team any questions you may have about the role, between 09.00am till 17.00pm Monday to Friday.
Shifts:Monday to Friday (37.5 hours)
Experience:
A relevant bachelor’s degree such as Life Sciences, Communications or Business Administration
About 5 years of experience in theBiopharmaceutical industry, with a preference for a background in marketing, demonstrating a successful track record in cross-functional project management
Demonstrable exposure to international, multiple brands and scientific fluency will be highly valued.
Strong collaborative skills, with the ability to engage effectively with stakeholders at various levels of the organisation to cultivate productive working relationships
Proven ability to coordinate multiple projects and deliver high-quality results in complex markets and therapeutic areas
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to convey information clearly and effectively
Adaptable and agile in response to change and new knowledge areas
Sense of urgency, ownership and problem-solving mindset
Strategic Thinking and Results-Driven: Capable of formulating strategic plans with a clear focus on achieving measurable results

Our client loves to reward their people for doing a great job.
This is a contract for 24 months.
A daily pay rate of IR35 £350.00 daily day Umbrella or £195.53 PAYE.
This role provides hybrid working access based in Central London.

If your application is successful, you’ll be involved in a live virtual interview with one of our client’s hiring managers to get to know you better.

json.jobTitle: Demand Generation & Rev's Ops Manager – Tech
json.description:
Your new company Our client is a high‑growth, VC-backed FinTech business. The organisation provides mission‑critical platforms to institutional clients in a highly regulated, global environment.Founded in recent years, it has scaled rapidly through product innovation and technical excellence. The business works with complex financial data, risk and compliance frameworks. It operates with a globally distributed team and a strong culture. This is an opportunity to join at a key growth stage with high visibility and impact.** This role does not offer sponsorship, you must have full right to work in the UK to be considered**Your new role They are now looking for a Demand Generation and RevOps Manager to oversee the GTM team’s operations. You'll sit within the marketing team's own analytics reporting, CRM coordination, event logistics, and the operational tasks that keep the marketing and growth team running.Key Responsibilities:• Lead and optimise multi-channel marketing campaigns to drive awareness, engagement, and pipeline growth• Oversee campaign performance measurement, reporting on leads, conversion, and commercial impact• Support the development and optimisation of marketing systems and processes to ensure accurate tracking and attribution• Design and manage scalable lead nurture and lifecycle campaigns aligned to customer journeys.• Maintain and optimise CRM processes to ensure marketing and sales alignment around lead quality and pipeline progression.• Collaborate with Sales on targeted account-based and decisions based on performance.• Confident using HubSpot.• Experience with conversion rate optimization or landing page testing.• Experience building email automation workflows (HubSpot, Brevo, or similar).• Strong organizational skills.• Proactive and resourceful.What you'll get in return • Remote working• Joining a scaling FinTech organisation• Grow a Marketing Function• Competitive Salary Your new company My client are a fast-growing eCommerce business in the animal health sector, providing pet owners with trusted veterinary medicines and wellbeing products through a highly rated online platform. Due to maternity, we are partnering to recruit the exciting job of Senior Digital Marketing Manager on a 9-12 month contract to manage the Digital team responsible for customer-facing online channels for their eCommerce store - including Loyalty, CRM & Marketing automation, SEO, Content, & Data Analytics. This is a full time, contract position working 3 days per week from their offices in Greater Bristol. Your new role As Senior Digital Marketing Manager, you will head up a talented team and support them with both strategy and operational guidance. You will be working in a very fast-paced e-commerce business where the team has a majority focus on the on-site customer journey and Retention/Loyalty activations, as well as working very closely with the Acquisition channels. You will be strategically developing a new loyalty programme, and shaping customer journeys based on behaviour and segmentation to personalise experiences on-site. As part of the SLT, you will operate in a hands-on leadership role, balancing strategy with delivery and driving the team to achieve measurable success across their channels. What you'll need to succeed You will need experience in an e-commerce business, comfortable with the DTC journey and driving the success of a transactional website. Experience of e-commerce trading and operations is advantageous, but not a pre-requisite as another team handles the day-to-day operations of the site. Instead, your expertise will be in the engagement of customers across the e-commerce customer journey, from SEO to Loyalty/Retention campaigns utilising Marketing Automation. You will have a broad experience across these channels, able to drive the execution of the team and get hands-on where required. You will have strong leadership and team management skills, and have operated as part of an SLT with solid stakeholder relationship skills. You'll be able to demonstrate an ability to work at pace, both creating strategy and driving growth through delivery. What you'll get in return You'll receive a salary of up to £65,000 (depending on experience) and a benefits package including 26 days' holiday + bank holidays, pension scheme, product discounts and enhanced family-friendly policies (salary and benefits will be pro rata if the contract runs for less than 12 months). You will work hybrid, spending 3 days a week at their offices in Greater Bristol, and be given the ability to work flexibly to meet the needs of the team and business. This is a great opportunity to join a strong brand during a period of positive change and growth. What you need to do now If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.  If this job isn't quite right for you but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

Your new companyA global consumer brand is seeking a Creative Effectiveness Manager to join its high-performing global marketing team. This role sits within a specialist marketing performance function and plays a key part in shaping how marketing effectiveness is measured, understood, and applied to future campaigns.Your new roleIn this role, you will be responsible for evaluating marketing and creative effectiveness by combining creative testing outputs from platforms such as System1 and Kantar with econometric modelling. You will translate complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that inform campaign development and creative decision-making.Key responsibilities:Analyse creative testing results alongside econometric modelling outputs, to assess creative strength, weaknesses, and commercial impactDeliver clear, compelling insight and recommendations to creative, marketing, and strategy stakeholders Influence campaign and creative decision-making through data-led storytelling Build strong, collaborative relationships across creative, insights, and marketing performance teams Develop and implement consistent creative and campaign effectiveness measurement frameworks Partner with adjacent insight and analytics teams to evolve long-term approaches to creative testing What you’ll need to succeedA strong analytical mindset with intermediate to advanced Excel skills Experience working with creative testing platforms such as System1 and/or Kantar, and econometric Marketing Mix Modelling outputs Proven ability to translate complex datasets into clear narratives and strategic recommendations Strong communication and presentation skills, with confidence influencing stakeholders at varying levels Experience working within marketing campaign and creative development processes A collaborative approach and comfortable working in cross-functional teams Experience within FMCG, a consumer brand, media agency, research, or creative agency environment is highly desirable. Exposure to multi-channel marketing across Owned, Earned, Shared and Paid channels is beneficial. Your new role
In this role, you will be responsible for evaluating marketing and creative effectiveness by combining creative testing outputs from platforms such as System1 and Kantar with econometric modelling. You will translate complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that inform campaign development and creative decision-making.
Key responsibilities:
Analyse creative testing results alongside econometric modelling outputs, to assess creative strength, weaknesses, and commercial impact
Deliver clear, compelling insight and recommendations to creative, marketing, and strategy stakeholders
Influence campaign and creative decision-making through data-led storytelling
Build strong, collaborative relationships across creative, insights, and marketing performance teams
Develop and implement consistent creative and campaign effectiveness measurement frameworks
Partner with adjacent insight and analytics teams to evolve long-term approaches to creative testing
What you’ll need to succeed
A strong analytical mindset with intermediate to advanced Excel skills
Experience working with creative testing platforms such as System1 and/or Kantar, and econometric Marketing Mix Modelling outputs
Proven ability to translate complex datasets into clear narratives and strategic recommendations
Strong communication and presentation skills, with confidence influencing stakeholders at varying levels
Experience working within marketing campaign and creative development processes
A collaborative approach and comfortable working in cross-functional teams
Experience within FMCG, a consumer brand, media agency, research, or creative agency environment is highly desirable. Exposure to multi-channel marketing across Owned, Earned, Shared and Paid channels is beneficial.
What you’ll get in return
The opportunity to work for a globally recognised brand with a strong focus on innovation and creativity
A role with genuine influence over how marketing performance and creative effectiveness are measured
Exposure to senior stakeholders and complex, high-profile campaigns
A collaborative, forward-thinking working environment

You will be responsible for all external events in the local communities of the Farnham and Reading areas, proactively reaching out to relevant organisations to arrange the charity's attendance at local events and community activities, to promote visibility of the charity’s work. You will help to coordinate event marketing, and contribute to campaigns to build awareness of the charity and its services, including posting on Social Media, creating compelling press releases, news stories, and other marketing activities. You will take an active role in reaching out within local communities to establish ways to grow local visibility and get involved in community events and activities – including public speaking.As part of a small team, you will work closely with the Marketing Manager and Community Liaison for Surrey and your responsibilities will be varied across community relations; seeking and creating PR opportunities, including news stories, supporting the marketing and recruitment into their volunteer programme, fundraising, and event coordination. This will require travel across Farnham and Reading, and occasional weekend work (time off during the week in lieu). What you'll need to succeed You could have a background in Events or PR, Fundraising, or perhaps even Business Development. We are seeking someone with the confidence and willingness to pick up the phone and engage with relevant people within local communities, as well as organising and attending events, speaking to members of the public and proactively building awareness of the charity's good work and services. You will have strong copywriting skills, and whilst you don't need previous PR experience, you will need to be willing to write news stories and make approaches to local media to gain coverage for the charity in the local press. An ideal background would be a mix of events, Social Media and communications, but with core requirements in event organisation, written skills, and people-facing/customer service, the charity is open to different backgrounds if you possess the core attributes required to be effective in the role. You must be a driver with access to your own transport as the role will require travel between care homes and local community events. What you'll get in return You will receive a full-time annual salary of up to £35,000 plus benefits including pension scheme, life assurance, Blue Light Card, and 28 days' holiday, rising to 33 in your second year. You will be based out of locations in either Farnham or Reading (depending on what is most convenient for you), but you must have your own transport and be able to commute to all locations regularly. Occasional weekend work will be required, and the role requires an on-site and community-based presence. Therefore, there are not a lot of opportunities for home working. We are recruiting for an experienced Category Manager.
This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated procurement professional with solid Public Sector experience to manage clinical categories, lead procurement activity, and work closely with clinicians and operational stakeholders to deliver value and service improvement.
The Role
Reporting to a Senior Category Manager, you will be responsible for managing designated clinical procurement categories, supporting tendering activity, contract management and supplier performance, and contributing to efficiency and savings programmes across partner organisations.
Key Responsibilities
Manage and deliver procurement activity across allocated clinical categories
Support and lead high‑value and complex tenders using e‑tendering systems
Manage and develop category work plans, aligned to national and collaborative priorities
Provide advice and support to clinical and non‑clinical stakeholders on procurement processes
Support product standardisation and supplier rationalisation initiatives
Undertake market research and keep abreast of technology and product developments
Manage supplier relationships and review contract performance
Ensure accurate recording and reporting of savings and cost pressures
Support compliance with UK Public Contract Regulations and internal governance
Essential requirements:
Significant experience working in NHS or similarly complex public‑sector procurement
Experience of clinical or acute hospital procurement categories
Good working knowledge of UK Public Contract Regulations
Experience of tendering, contract award and supplier negotiations
Strong analytical, organisational and communication skills
Ability to work independently and manage a varied workload
CIPS qualified (part or full) or willing to work towards qualification
Desirable:
Experience working across multiple organisations or collaborative procurement arrangements
Experience of e‑tendering systems and clinical product evaluations
What’s on Offer
Salary: NHS Band 7 plus applicable High Cost Area Supplement (where relevant)
Generous annual leave entitlement, increasing with service
NHS Pension Scheme
Hybrid working arrangements
Ongoing professional development and CIPS support
Opportunity to work on clinically meaningful, high‑impact procurement projects

json.jobTitle: Senior Solicitor – Private Wealth & Inheritance (4+ PQE)
json.description:
Your new company An award-winning Legal 500 firm, recognised across multiple areas that continues to experience exceptional growth, having doubled in size in recent years. With ambitious plans to keep expanding, the firm offers clear progression routes for talented solicitors at every stage of their career. Its meritocratic culture ensures transparent goals for promotion and well-defined career pathways, supported by strong leadership and mentoring opportunities.Working from a single, vibrant office, you’ll enjoy a collaborative, energetic environment with open-plan spaces designed to encourage teamwork and innovation. The firm combines high performance with balance, offering hybrid flexibility, realistic targets, and a generous bonus structure that rewards hard work. If you’re an ambitious, qualified solicitor looking to join a dynamic, forward-thinking practice, this is an opportunity to progress your career. Due to continued growth, we are looking to engage a Senior Solicitor within the Private Wealth & Inheritance team.Your new role As a key member of our Private Wealth & Inheritance team, you’ll play a pivotal role in delivering exceptional client service and shaping the future of the department. This is a chance to join a high-performing team within a firm that values growth, collaboration, and excellence.You will:Manage a varied caseload of private client matters, including wills, trusts, probate, inheritance tax planning, and estate administration.Advise clients on complex inheritance and tax matters, ensuring their wealth is protected for future generations.Work closely with other departments, such as family law and property, to provide a comprehensive service.Lead and support junior team members through guidance, mentoring, and oversight.Build and maintain strong client relationships through excellent communication and a personal approach.Contribute to business development activities, including networking and marketing initiatives.What you'll need to succeed We’re looking for someone who is:Qualified with 4+ years PQE in Private Wealth, Inheritance, or a related field.Technically strong in wills, trusts, probate, and tax planning.Skilled in client care, with the ability to handle sensitive matters with discretion and empathy.Organised and capable of managing a busy caseload effectively.STEP qualified or willing to work towards it (desirable but not essential).What you'll get in return A competitive salary based on experience partnered with a generous fee-earner bonus scheme. The office has free parking and is easily accessible. They provide a comprehensive benefits package including pension, enhanced maternity/paternity pay, holiday etc. This firm will give you the opportunity to learn and grow within a supportive and dynamic team, whilst developing leadership opportunities through their progressive development program.What you need to do now If you are interested in these roles or would like further information, please get in touch for a confidential discussion.If these jobs aren’t quite right for you, but you’re exploring new opportunities, we’d still love to hear from you.We would expect a lawyer with the given PQE to have gained the level of experience required, but this does not preclude applications from those with more or less PQE.
Your new company
Working from a single, vibrant office, you’ll enjoy a collaborative, energetic environment with open-plan spaces designed to encourage teamwork and innovation. The firm combines high performance with balance, offering hybrid flexibility, realistic targets, and a generous bonus structure that rewards hard work. If you’re an ambitious, qualified solicitor looking to join a dynamic, forward-thinking practice, this is an opportunity to progress your career. Due to continued growth, we are looking to engage a Senior Solicitor within the Private Wealth & Inheritance team.
Your new role
As a key member of our Private Wealth & Inheritance team, you’ll play a pivotal role in delivering exceptional client service and shaping the future of the department. This is a chance to join a high-performing team within a firm that values growth, collaboration, and excellence.
You will:
Manage a varied caseload of private client matters, including wills, trusts, probate, inheritance tax planning, and estate administration.
Advise clients on complex inheritance and tax matters, ensuring their wealth is protected for future generations.
Work closely with other departments, such as family law and property, to provide a comprehensive service.
Lead and support junior team members through guidance, mentoring, and oversight.
Build and maintain strong client relationships through excellent communication and a personal approach.
Contribute to business development activities, including networking and marketing initiatives.
What you'll need to succeed
Qualified with 4+ years PQE in Private Wealth, Inheritance, or a related field.
Technically strong in wills, trusts, probate, and tax planning.
Skilled in client care, with the ability to handle sensitive matters with discretion and empathy.
Organised and capable of managing a busy caseload effectively.
STEP qualified or willing to work towards it (desirable but not essential).
What you'll get in return
What you need to do now
If you are interested in these roles or would like further information, please get in touch for a confidential discussion.
If these jobs aren’t quite right for you, but you’re exploring new opportunities, we’d still love to hear from you.
We would expect a lawyer with the given PQE to have gained the level of experience required, but this does not preclude applications from those with more or less PQE.

We are real living employees.Competitive Company Pension Scheme.Health cash plan scheme.Professional membership supportFamily policiesGreat office team building events and fully expensed seasonal social events.Cycle to work Scheme.On-site parking facilities (excluding London office)Season Ticket Loan (if applicable)Death in service benefit.Early finish incentives when the practice targets are hit.Additional leave is allocated for volunteering (up to 2 days).Health & Mental Wellness Programs.Employee Referral Scheme.Employee working groupsWhat you need to do now  If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.  If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
Senior/ Principal Mechanical Design Engineer (Building Services)
Salary up to £75,000
The role:
We are currently recruiting a Senior/ Principal Mechanical Engineer to join our Building Services team in our Billericay office.
The successful candidate will be responsible for providing expertise in M&E by means of economical, safe, and sustainable systems. The team focusses on projects in the Healthcare and Education sectors.
They will be expected to work in accordance with the practice’s values and objectives, ensuring effective communication and engagement with clients and key stakeholders. They must ensure their own professional competence through continued professional development (CPD) and training, as well as take responsibility for mentoring and coaching of M&E team members as required.
The Responsibility:
Work with the Engineering Associate/Director on a regular basis, reporting weekly on progress.
Ensure all projects are completed to a high technical standard, on time and on budget.
Assist more junior staff with technical queries as appropriate to your knowledge and experience.
Carry out fee-earning work, getting involved in the design of schemes where appropriate and particularly where high-level technical expertise is required.
Undertake feasibility studies as required.
Meet with clients to establish project briefs and budget costs for client consideration.
Draw up detailed specifications and drawings against client’s briefs.
Review tenders received and prepare tender reports and recommendations.
Project manage site works from commencement to completion.
Agents work against specification requirements and agree with a contractor’s final accounts.
Carry out inspections of existing installations and submit condition reports.
Check and prepare information for fee Invoicing each month.
Manage the day-to-day workload of individuals reporting directly to you, ensuring clarity of their roles, motivation of the team and wellbeing of individuals.
Advise director and partners on resources required, assisting at interview stage.
Develop the client base of the practice by being proactive in pursuing marketing opportunities that arise and pursuing marketing initiatives as directed.
Benefits:
Opportunities for flexible and remote work – we understand a work-life balance helps us all.
Holiday entitlement of 25 days plus bank holidays with long service recognition and festive shutdown.
Opportunities for personal and professional growth in a supportive environment, including in-house coaching and mentoring.
Competitive salaries with regular review and benchmarking to ensure fair pay. We are real living employees.
Competitive Company Pension Scheme.
Health cash plan scheme.
Professional membership support
Family policies
Great office team building events and fully expensed seasonal social events.
Cycle to work Scheme.
On-site parking facilities (excluding London office)
Season Ticket Loan (if applicable)
Death in service benefit.
Early finish incentives when the practice targets are hit.
Additional leave is allocated for volunteering (up to 2 days).
Health & Mental Wellness Programs.
Employee Referral Scheme.
Employee working groups
What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.
If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

You will liaise closely with Customer Service, IT, Digital and Commercial teams to ensure smooth operations and continuous improvement. You will act as the key point of contact for digital tools across the region, supporting internal users, managing technical queries and helping evolve B2B e-commerce platforms. Responsibilities include troubleshooting issues, managing user access, updating platform content, supporting new functionality rollouts and documenting enhancements.
